# Prayer Times

> A multi-city Salah coordination app for finding prayer-safe meeting windows across timezones.

Prayer Times is a production single-file web app at https://prayer.mscarabia.com. It shows prayer times for multiple cities on a 24-hour timeline, highlights conflicts, supports personal tasks, exports iCal calendars, and helps coordinate Quran class schedules around Salah.

## Canonical URLs

- Live app: https://prayer.mscarabia.com
- Repository: https://github.com/defaltadmin/world-prayer-times

## Current architecture

- Main app: `index.html`
- Service worker: `sw.js`
- PWA manifest: `manifest.json`
- Meeting links Worker: `worker/index.js`
- Configuration/docs: `CONTEXT.md`, `PRD.md`

## Product features

- Multi-city prayer timeline
- Aladhan API prayer times
- Nominatim city search
- Prayer-safe meeting-window selection
- Conflict detection
- Personal tasks on the timeline
- LocalStorage persistence
- iCal export with VTIMEZONE support
- Quran class timetable
- Password-gated Teams meeting links via Cloudflare Worker
- Browser notification scheduling
- Dark/light theme
- Arabic RTL support
- PWA install support

## Security model

Meeting links are stored in a Cloudflare Worker and returned only after the raw course password matches `env.COURSE_SECRET` using timing-safe comparison. The public frontend hash is only a local UI gate and is not the real authorization boundary.

## Design system

The app uses a teal/cyan prayer-time interface with IBM Plex Sans Arabic, JetBrains Mono for time values, glass panels, and a dark timeline optimized for fast scheduling.

Primary palette:
- Surface: `#091117`
- Accent: `#2dd4bf`
- Accent 2: `#06b6d4`
- Safe: `#34d399`
- Danger: `#f87171`

## Quality checks

For changes:
- Test the app on desktop, tablet, and mobile widths
- Verify city search and prayer API fetches
- Verify iCal imports into Google Calendar and Outlook
- Verify task CRUD persists after reload
- Verify meeting links Worker returns links only with the active secret
